digest.greeting: Jean, today narrowed the Staff Aug and Mental Health follow-up work while leaving the leadership storyline, KPI goals, timecards, Billing intake, SLQ, and Incident Response ownership path as the main open threads. Tomorrow has several useful checkpoints, including the Jessica and Jhara 1:1s, the LeadUp/Yuri overlap, and the intake workflow meeting.

items.label: Closed the Staff Aug touchpoint follow-up
items.detail: The TRACE & Staff Aug follow-up that asked whether any support was still needed is now marked complete after today's touchpoint path.

items.label: Prepared the Mental Health project touchpoint
items.detail: The Mental Health project preparation item is now complete after being carried into today's afternoon scope and next-step discussion.

items.label: Finish the EPMO storyline for leadership use
items.detail: Keep the story tight around proof points, current risks, and what leadership needs to decide or unblock across Staff Augmentation, Send Letters, CoE metrics, and active intake movement.

items.label: Turn the KPI set into SMART goals
items.detail: Convert the KPI set into a small set of clear goals that can support the EPMO storyline and PM/PE follow-through.

items.label: Approve pending Rippling timecards
items.detail: The morning digest flagged multiple Rippling alerts tied to pending timecards and the Monday payroll cutoff. Keep this visible until the timecards are confirmed approved.

items.label: Route the new Billing intake
items.detail: The Billing intake from Shannon Cooper still needs routing clarity and a next owner if it did not move during Portfolio Review.

items.label: Confirm the SLQ 2.0 Baesman path
items.detail: Use the latest packaging details from the Baesman thread to confirm what is still needed from Procurement, IT, or the project team after today's Send Letters Queue checkpoint.

items.label: Clarify Bucket 6 ownership risk for Incident Response
items.detail: Lucia can try to fill the reputation/brand matrix next week but is not fully involved in PR right now. Decide whether to wait, assign an interim reviewer, or narrow what is needed for the June 30 draft path.

items.label: David Molano's June 26 leave was approved
items.detail: Rippling notified Jean that David Molano's leave for June 26 was approved, with the standard note that team members should fully disconnect during leave.

items.label: 1:1 Jessica and Jean
items.detail: Useful follow-up point for Case Change or process-map delivery if Jessica's Tuesday deliverables need a checkpoint.

items.label: 1:1 Jhara & Jean
items.detail: Agenda names portfolio review, roadblocks, and next steps. Good place to pressure-test SLQ, Mental Health, and active PM work.

items.label: Blocked Space
items.detail: Keep this available for EPMO storyline cleanup or follow-through from today's Portfolio Review.

items.label: bi-weekly sync (J & J)
items.detail: Workload, challenges, wins, and goals sync with Janet McDowell.

items.label: LeadUp Module 2 and 1:1 Yuri overlap
items.detail: Outlook shows LeadUp Module 2 and 1:1 Yuri & Jean starting at the same time. Decide coverage before tomorrow afternoon.

items.label: Intakes workflow meeting
items.detail: Janet's invite says the meeting will cover the proposed workflow for routing department projects and BA requests through the Pending IT Review path.
